channel.title: Media on Te Moana Mahana
channel.description: Recent content in Media on Te Moana Mahana

item.title: Sculpture on the Gulf 2027
item.description: Gabby O’Connor is an artist and scientist who is also one of our team members who is working on within the “Ocean Connections” project. Recently, she was chosen as one of the artists to help contribute a scultpure for the Sculpture on the Gulf 2027 exhibition, which is held biennially on the coastal headland of Matiatia Bay, Waiheke Island. This year, it is being held from the 26th Feburary to the 29th March 2027.

item.title: Why Ocean Heat Content is Important for Weather
item.description: Kevin Trenberth, a distinguished scholar and climate scientist, recently wrote an article for The Conversation on how record ocean heat content is a key factor in the current wet weather that faces New Zealand every summer.

item.title: Interview about the project
item.description: Our principal investigator, Melissa Bowen, was recently interviewed about the project on RNZ Afternoons with Jesse Mulligan.
